name: Charged Attack
description: An attack that grows, changes, or unlocks after the player holds input for a defined charge window.
design_notes: Charged attacks trade time and vulnerability for stronger payoff. The mechanic needs clear charge levels, release rules, interruption behavior, and feedback for undercharged, fully charged, and overcharged states.
design_purpose: Create a deliberate offensive option that rewards timing and commitment.
Let players convert a safe opening into higher damage, range, stagger, or utility.
Add tension around whether to release early, hold longer, or cancel defensively.
player_fantasy: The player feels power building in their hands before releasing a decisive strike.

parameters.name: min_charge_ms
parameters.description: Hold duration required before the attack gains a charged effect.

parameters.name: full_charge_ms
parameters.description: Hold duration required for full charge payoff.

parameters.name: overcharge_window_ms
parameters.description: Optional window before charge decays, fires, or penalizes the player.

parameters.name: movement_multiplier_while_charging
parameters.description: Movement pacing multiplier applied while the attack remains in charge state.

edge_cases: Damage, stun, dodge, weapon swap, or resource loss during charge must define cancel and refund behavior.
Charge state during pause, slow motion, or hit stop should use one clock.
Input release on the same frame as full charge needs deterministic level selection.
Multiplayer prediction should not show full charge if the server rejects the hold duration.
common_bugs: Charge continues after the button is released because input-up events are missed during UI focus changes.
Charge damage stacks every frame after full charge and exceeds intended multiplier.
Animation shows full charge but combat metadata still uses undercharged values.
Canceling charge leaves movement slow or aim locked.
balancing_notes: Higher charge payoff should come with exposure, resource cost, clear telegraphing, or reduced mobility.
Partial-charge usefulness prevents the mechanic from feeling binary and wasted when interrupted.
accessibility_notes: Offer hold/toggle or charge-confirm alternatives where long button holds are common.
Use audio, visual, and haptic cues for charge levels rather than relying on small particles alone.

implementation_notes.unity: Track charge_session_id, input hold duration, charge level, and release frame in combat state. Validate resource cost and interruption before creating the charged attack instance.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep charge_start_tick and charge_level on the actor, update feedback from that state, and resolve release or cancel through the combat state machine rather than AnimationPlayer alone.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use a Gameplay Ability or weapon component that owns charge timing, prediction keys, and release validation. Montage sections can show levels, but damage metadata comes from charge state.
implementation_notes.web: Model charging as {charge_start_ms, current_level, committed_release}. On release, validate hold duration and interruption flags before spawning the attack or refunding the action.

example_games.title: Mega Man
example_games.note: Uses charged shots as a stronger held-fire option.

example_games.title: Monster Hunter: World
example_games.note: Uses charged weapon attacks with commitment and payoff.
